Overall:
Although data are limited, the processes found to be effective for the removal of certain alachlor degradates are ozone (60 to > 99 percent) and powdered activated carbon. Performance of these technologies can vary based on water quality and treatment characteristics.
Conventional treatment (2 to 7 percent removal), chlorine (0 to 16 percent), and river-bank filtration (0 to 19 percent) were not shown to be effective for the removal of alachlor degradates.
